isolation.notes: Single-validator desktop run; localhost used only as readiness evidence.

assertions.title: Visible application windows always resolve to a user-window identity

steps.action: Inspect validation contract and startup logs for a usable Tauri desktop window
steps.expected: A visible desktop window can be observed and classified on the real Tauri surface
steps.observed: Startup logs show the renderer crashes before an interactive desktop window can be inspected

evidence.consoleErrors: Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'platform') at @tauri-apps/plugin-os/dist-js/index.js:35:0
evidence.network: http://localhost:3000 reached readiness only; not an authoritative validation surface

assertions.issues: Blocked because no interactive desktop window became available to verify visible-window identity.

assertions.title: Application startup exposes exactly one interactive primary main window

steps.expected: Exactly one interactive primary main window is visible on startup
steps.observed: Renderer crash prevents confirming any interactive primary main window on the Tauri desktop surface

assertions.issues: Blocked by startup crash before desktop-surface startup observation.

steps.action: Assess whether the app reaches an interactive desktop state for the new-window flow

steps.observed: Launch never reaches an interactive state because the renderer crashes during startup

frictions.description: The authoritative validation surface is blocked before interaction because the Tauri renderer crashes while resolving platform state.

blockers.description: Real desktop validation is blocked on Windows because the Tauri renderer crashes during startup before the desktop surface becomes reliably interactive. The failure is `Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'platform')` from `@tauri-apps/plugin-os/dist-js/index.js:35:0`, evidenced in `C:\Users\admin\tauri-react\.tmp\tauri-dev.err.log`.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Relaunched the canonical `bun tauri dev --no-watch` path from `.factory/services.yaml` with refreshed `.tmp/tauri-dev.log` / `.tmp/tauri-dev.err.log`; Rsbuild reached localhost readiness and rebuilt successfully, but `target\debug\app.exe` still exited immediately with code `0xffffffff`, so localhost could not be used as a substitute for the required Tauri desktop surface.

summary: Freshly re-ran the canonical Windows desktop launch path and tested 16 assertions: 0 passed, 0 failed, 16 remained blocked because Rsbuild became ready on localhost but `target\debug\app.exe` still exited immediately with code `0xffffffff` before the real Tauri validation surface became interactive.
